message.content:
Read these files and return their full contents:
1. /home/ketan/project/kapow/.beads/config.yaml
2. /home/ketan/project/kapow/CLAUDE.md (the beads-related sections)
3. /home/ketan/project/kapow/AGENTS.md
4. /home/ketan/project/kapow/Makefile (just the top-level targets)

Also check if there are any .claude/skills/ files related to beads in kapow.

content.text: I'll read those files for you. Let me start by checking the directory structure and reading the specified files.

content.content:
1‚Üí# Beads Configuration File
     2‚Üí# This file configures default behavior for all bd commands in this repository
     3‚Üí# All settings can also be set via environment variables (BD_* prefix)
     4‚Üí# or overridden with command-line flags
     5‚Üí
     6‚Üí# Issue prefix for this repository (used by bd init)
     7‚Üí# If not set, bd init will auto-detect from directory name
     8‚Üí# Example: issue-prefix: "myproject" creates issues like "myproject-1", "myproject-2", etc.
     9‚Üí# issue-prefix: ""
    10‚Üí
    11‚Üí# Use no-db mode: load from JSONL, write back after each command
    12‚Üí# When true, bd will use .beads/issues.jsonl as the source of truth
    13‚Üí# instead of the Dolt database
    14‚Üíno-db: true
    15‚Üí
    16‚Üí# Enable JSON output by default
    17‚Üí# json: false
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí# Default actor for audit trails (overridden by BD_ACTOR or --actor)
    20‚Üí# actor: ""
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí# Debounce interval for auto-flush (can also use BEADS_FLUSH_DEBOUNCE)
    23‚Üí# flush-debounce: "5s"
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üí# Export events (audit trail) to .beads/events.jsonl on each flush/sync
    26‚Üí# When enabled, new events are appended incrementally using a high-water mark.
    27‚Üí# Use 'bd export --events' to trigger manually regardless of this setting.
    28‚Üí# events-export: false
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí# Git branch for beads commits (bd sync will commit to this branch)
    31‚Üí# IMPORTANT: Set this for team projects so all clones use the same sync branch.
    32‚Üí# This setting persists across clones (unlike database config which is gitignored).
    33‚Üí# Can also use BEADS_SYNC_BRANCH env var for local override.
    34‚Üí# If not set, bd sync will require you to run 'bd config set sync.branch <branch>'.
    35‚Üí# sync-branch: "beads-sync"
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí# Multi-repo configuration (experimental - bd-307)
    38‚Üí# Allows hydrating from multiple repositories and routing writes to the correct JSONL
    39‚Üí# repos:
    40‚Üí#   primary: "."  # Primary repo (where this database lives)
    41‚Üí#   additional:   # Additional repos to hydrate from (read-only)
    42‚Üí#     - ~/beads-planning  # Personal planning repo
    43‚Üí#     - ~/work-planning   # Work planning repo
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí# Integration settings (access with 'bd config get/set')
    46‚Üí# These are stored in the database, not in this file:
    47‚Üí# - jira.url
    48‚Üí# - jira.project
    49‚Üí# - linear.url
    50‚Üí# - linear.api-key
    51‚Üí# - github.org
    52‚Üí# - github.repo
    53‚Üí

1‚Üí# Kapow ‚Äî Claude Instructions
     2‚Üí
     3‚ÜíCollege Power Search: a monorepo with a Go API (`api/`) and a React/TypeScript
     4‚Üífrontend (`web/`). Component-specific deep-dives are in the `@import` files below.
     5‚Üí
     6‚Üí---
     7‚Üí
     8‚Üí## Prerequisites
     9‚Üí
    10‚Üí- **Go** 1.23+
    11‚Üí- **Node.js** 22+ with **pnpm** 9+ (`npm install -g pnpm`)
    12‚Üí- **Docker** (for `make dev` ‚Äî runs PostgreSQL in a container)
    13‚Üí- **PostgreSQL** 15+ with PostGIS (only if managing DB manually instead of `make dev`)
    14‚Üí- **golangci-lint** (optional): see golangci-lint docs
    15‚Üí
    16‚Üí---
    17‚Üí
    18‚Üí## Environment setup
    19‚Üí
    20‚Üí```bash
    21‚Üícp .env.example .env   # fill in DATABASE_URL and JWT_SECRET at minimum
    22‚Üí```
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíRequired: `DATABASE_URL` ‚Äî the API refuses to start without it.
    25‚Üí
    26‚ÜíPorts:
    27‚Üí- **API**: `:8081`
    28‚Üí- **Web dev server**: `:8080` ‚Äî proxies `/graphql` and `/api` ‚Üí `localhost:8081`
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí---
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí## Commands (Root Makefile)
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí| Command | Action |
    35‚Üí|---|---|
    36‚Üí| `make dev` | Full-stack dev environment (Docker PG + air + Vite) ‚Äî single command |
    37‚Üí| `make dev-clean` | Remove dev DB volume (forces fresh fixture load) |
    38‚Üí| `make dev-prune` | Remove kapow-dev volumes older than 30 days |
    39‚Üí| `make api-build` | Compile `bin/kapow-api` |
    40‚Üí| `make api-run` | Run API server |
    41‚Üí| `make api-generate` | Regenerate gqlgen code |
    42‚Üí| `make api-test` | All Go tests (race detector) |
    43‚Üí| `make api-test-unit` | Unit tests only (`-short`) |
    44‚Üí| `make lint` | Run all linters (Go + web) |
    45‚Üí| `make audit` | Security audit on web dependencies |
    46‚Üí| `make api-lint` | go vet + golangci-lint (includes gosec) |
    47‚Üí| `make api-migrate-up` | Apply pending migrations |
    48‚Üí| `make api-migrate-down` | Roll back last migration |
    49‚Üí| `make schema-gen` | Rebuild `common/.../schema.sql` |
    50‚Üí| `make web-install` | `pnpm install` |
    51‚Üí| `make web-dev` | Vite dev server (port 8080) |
    52‚Üí| `make web-build` | TypeScript check + production build |
    53‚Üí| `make web-lint` | ESLint (zero-warning mode) |
    54‚Üí| `make web-test` | Vitest unit tests |
    55‚Üí| `make web-schema-sync` | Regenerate `web/schema.graphql` + `graphql-env.d.ts` from API SDL |
    56‚Üí| `make web-clean` | Remove `dist/` and `node_modules/` |
    57‚Üí| `make web-test-e2e` | All Playwright E2E tests (auto-starts dev server) |
    58‚Üí| `make web-test-e2e-critical` | Only `@critical` tier E2E tests |
    59‚Üí| `make smoke-test` | Search API smoke test (requires running API) |
    60‚Üí| `make test-fixture` | Validate fixture data loads against current schema (temp Docker PG) |
    61‚Üí| `make test-quick` | Fast checks: Go unit tests + TS build + ESLint (~10-20s, no DB) |
    62‚Üí| `make test-standard` | Full local: quick + web unit tests + Go lint (~30-60s, no DB) |
    63‚Üí| `make test-full` | Complete: standard + integration + e2e + smoke + fixture (requires DB + services) |
    64‚Üí| `make test-image-build` | Build all-in-one Docker test image |
    65‚Üí| `make test-image-run` | Run the Docker test image |
    66‚Üí| `make spec-gen` | Regenerate `docs/specs/generated/` reference docs |
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üí---
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üí## Testing standards
    71‚Üí
    72‚Üí### Test tiers
    73‚Üí
    74‚Üí| Tier | Command | Time | External deps | Use when |
    75‚Üí|---|---|---|---|---|
    76‚Üí| Quick | `make test-quick` | ~10-20s | None | During development |
    77‚Üí| Standard | `make test-standard` | ~30-60s | None | Before committing |
    78‚Üí| Full | `make test-full` | ~2-5min | DB + running services | Before merge |
    79‚Üí
    80‚Üí### Core rules
    81‚Üí
    82‚Üí- **Always write tests for new additions.** Tests ship with the code, not as a follow-up.
    83‚Üí- **Update existing tests when modifying behaviour.**
    84‚Üí- **Target ‚â• 80% line coverage per package.** Glue/wiring packages (`cmd/server`, `router`) are exempt.
    85‚Üí- **Always run database code against a real database.** Compilation and unit tests alone are not sufficient ‚Äî runtime issues like pgx type encoding mismatches only surface with a live connection.
    86‚Üí
    87‚Üí---
    88‚Üí
    89‚Üí## Issue tracking (Beads)
    90‚Üí
    91‚Üí```bash
    92‚Üíbd list                                # all open issues
    93‚Üíbd ready                               # issues with no blockers (ready to work)
    94‚Üíbd show <id>                           # detailed view with dependencies
    95‚Üíbd create --title "..." --description "..."
    96‚Üíbd update <id> --status in_progress
    97‚Üíbd close <id>
    98‚Üí```
    99‚Üí
   100‚ÜíSee `AGENTS.md` for issue type guidance, epic creation, dependencies, and
   101‚Üícompletion workflow.
   102‚Üí
   103‚Üí---
   104‚Üí
   105‚Üí## Common task recipes
   106‚Üí
   107‚Üí### Add a config env var
   108‚Üí
   109‚Üí1. Add field to `Config` struct in `api/internal/config/config.go` with `env:"VAR_NAME"` tag
   110‚Üí2. Document it in `.env.example`
   111‚Üí3. Access via `cfg.FieldName` (config flows through `router.Deps`)
   112‚Üí
   113‚Üí### Add a database query
   114‚Üí
   115‚ÜíUse pgx directly: `pool.QueryRow(ctx, sql, $1, $2, ...)`. Always pass context.
   116‚ÜíNever build SQL with `fmt.Sprintf` ‚Äî always use `$N` placeholders.
   117‚Üí
   118‚Üí### Add a Go package
   119‚Üí
   120‚ÜíCreate `api/internal/<name>/` with `package <name>`. Pass deps explicitly via
   121‚Üístructs; no `init()` globals.
   122‚Üí
   123‚Üí---
   124‚Üí
   125‚Üí## Git workflow
   126‚Üí
   127‚ÜíWhen work is complete and verified, merge directly into `main` from the command
   128‚Üíline. Do not create pull requests unless there is an unresolvable merge problem.
   129‚Üí
   130‚ÜíSee `AGENTS.md` for commit conventions, worktree workflow, and session
   131‚Üícompletion protocol.
   132‚Üí
   133‚Üí---
   134‚Üí
   135‚Üí## Implementation plans
   136‚Üí
   137‚ÜíAfter plan mode approval, copy the plan from `~/.claude/plans/` into
   138‚Üí`docs/plans/` with a meaningful name (e.g., `kapow-a1b-geo-search.md`).
   139‚ÜíPlans in `~/.claude/` are machine-local; the repo copy syncs across devices.
   140‚ÜíSee `AGENTS.md` for naming conventions and lifecycle.
   141‚Üí
   142‚Üí---
   143‚Üí
   144‚Üí## Project specs (`docs/specs/`)
   145‚Üí
   146‚ÜíComprehensive product and architecture documentation lives in `docs/specs/`.
   147‚ÜíConsult these before making significant changes to understand current behaviour.
   148‚Üí
   149‚Üí**Hand-written specs** (edit when behaviour changes):
   150‚Üí
   151‚Üí| File | Covers |
   152‚Üí|---|---|
   153‚Üí| `product-overview.md` | What Kapow is, features, user stories, pages |
   154‚Üí| `graphql-api.md` | Queries, mutations, types, auth requirements |
   155‚Üí| `search-engine.md` | Field types, SQL generation, themes, data sources |
   156‚Üí| `data-model.md` | Schemas, tables, relationships, migrations |
   157‚Üí| `authentication.md` | JWT flow, Supabase, middleware, protected routes |
   158‚Üí| `frontend-architecture.md` | Pages, components, state, GraphQL client, styling |
   159‚Üí| `deployment.md` | Docker images, compose, env vars, startup sequence |
   160‚Üí
   161‚Üí**Auto-generated reference** (`docs/specs/generated/` ‚Äî regenerate with `make spec-gen`):
   162‚Üí
   163‚Üí| File | Source |
   164‚Üí|---|---|
   165‚Üí| `search-fields.md` | `search.NewRegistry()` ‚Äî all fields grouped by theme |
   166‚Üí| `data-sources.md` | `search.AllDataSources()` ‚Äî all 12 data sources |
   167‚Üí| `graphql-schema.md` | `api/graph/schema/*.graphql` ‚Äî full SDL |
   168‚Üí| `env-reference.md` | `.env.example` ‚Äî all environment variables |
   169‚Üí
   170‚Üí---
   171‚Üí
   172‚Üí## What NOT to do
   173‚Üí
   174‚Üí- **Never edit** `graph/generated/generated.go` or `graph/model/models_gen.go` ‚Äî run `make api-generate`
   175‚Üí- **Never commit** `.env` ‚Äî only `.env.example`
   176‚Üí- **Never use `fmt.Sprintf` to build SQL** ‚Äî use parameterized `$N` placeholders
   177‚Üí- **Never hardcode brand colors** ‚Äî use Mantine color refs (`c="brand.9"`, `color="accent"`) or CSS vars (`var(--color-primary)` / `var(--color-accent)`)
   178‚Üí- **Never use Tailwind utility classes** ‚Äî use Mantine style props or inline styles
   179‚Üí- **Never add** `node_modules/` or `dist/` to git
   180‚Üí
   181‚Üí---
   182‚Üí
   183‚Üí## README.md
   184‚Üí
   185‚Üí`README.md` is the human-facing project documentation. **Keep it up to date**
   186‚Üíwhen making changes that affect how someone builds, runs, or configures the
   187‚Üíproject.
   188‚Üí
   189‚Üí---
   190‚Üí
   191‚Üí@api/CLAUDE.md
   192‚Üí@web/CLAUDE.md
   193‚Üí@tools/CLAUDE.md
   194‚Üí

1‚Üí# Agent Instructions
     2‚Üí
     3‚ÜíThis project uses **bd** (beads) for issue tracking. Run `bd onboard` to get started.
     4‚Üí
     5‚Üí## Quick Reference
     6‚Üí
     7‚Üí```bash
     8‚Üíbd ready              # Find available work
     9‚Üíbd show <id>          # View issue details
    10‚Üíbd update <id> --status in_progress  # Claim work
    11‚Üíbd close <id>         # Complete work
    12‚Üíbd sync               # Sync with git
    13‚Üí```
    14‚Üí
    15‚Üí## Issue Title Guidelines
    16‚Üí
    17‚ÜíTitles appear in `bd list`, `bd ready`, and terminal window titles where space
    18‚Üíis limited. The **first ~20 characters** must be meaningfully descriptive:
    19‚Üí
    20‚Üí| Bad | Good |
    21‚Üí|---|---|
    22‚Üí| `Add support for filtering by SAT score` | `SAT score filter for search` |
    23‚Üí| `Update the search page to show result counts` | `Search result count display` |
    24‚Üí| `Fix bug where geographic radius is ignored` | `Geo radius filter ignored` |
    25‚Üí| `Implement new card component for institutions` | `Institution card component` |
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí- **Lead with the noun or area** ("Search ‚Ä¶", "Auth ‚Ä¶", "GeoField ‚Ä¶"), not filler verbs
    28‚Üí- **Under 50 characters** ‚Äî put detail in `--description`
    29‚Üí- Avoid prefixes like "Add support for", "Update the", "Implement new"
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí## Creating Issues
    32‚Üí
    33‚ÜíUse the correct issue type:
    34‚Üí- `epic` ‚Äî a grouping container for related features/tasks. Not directly workable.
    35‚Üí- `feature` ‚Äî delivers new user-visible or agent-visible capability
    36‚Üí- `task` ‚Äî scaffold, chore, or infrastructure work (e.g., project init, CI setup)
    37‚Üí- `bug` ‚Äî something broken that needs fixing
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí### Creating Epics
    40‚Üí
    41‚ÜíEpics must use `--waits-for-gate` so they are blocked until their children complete, and do not appear in `bd ready`:
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí```bash
    44‚Üíbd create "Epic: Feature Area" -t epic -p 2 -l label1,label2 \
    45‚Üí  -d "Description of the epic" \
    46‚Üí  --waits-for-gate all-children
    47‚Üí```
    48‚Üí
    49‚ÜíThis makes the epic resolve automatically when all children are closed. Never leave an epic as a bare open issue with no gate ‚Äî it will pollute `bd ready`.
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí### Creating Child Issues Under Epics
    52‚Üí
    53‚ÜíUse `--parent` to place issues under an epic, and `--waits-for` to make the epic wait for the child:
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí```bash
    56‚Üíbd create "Implement feature X" -t feature -p 1 -l search,go \
    57‚Üí  --parent kapow-abc \
    58‚Üí  --waits-for kapow-abc \
    59‚Üí  -d "Description" \
    60‚Üí  --acceptance "Acceptance criteria"
    61‚Üí```
    62‚Üí
    63‚ÜíThe `--parent` flag establishes the hierarchy. The `--waits-for kapow-abc` flag tells the epic to wait for this child before it can close.
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üí### Dependencies Between Issues
    66‚Üí
    67‚ÜíUse `bd dep add` to express ordering constraints between issues:
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí```bash
    70‚Üíbd dep add kapow-child kapow-dependency   # kapow-child is blocked by kapow-dependency
    71‚Üí```
    72‚Üí
    73‚ÜíOnly add dependencies where there is a real technical ordering constraint (e.g., "the migration must exist before the resolver can query the new column"). Do not add dependencies for soft preferences or nice-to-have ordering.
    74‚Üí
    75‚Üí## Implementation Plans
    76‚Üí
    77‚ÜíClaude Code saves plans to `~/.claude/plans/` with random names (e.g.,
    78‚Üí`replicated-drifting-meerkat.md`). These are local to the machine and lost
    79‚Üíwhen switching devices. **After a plan is approved, copy it into the repo**
    80‚Üíso it persists across machines and sessions.
    81‚Üí
    82‚Üí### Saving a plan to the repo
    83‚Üí
    84‚ÜíAfter exiting plan mode, copy the plan file from `~/.claude/plans/` into
    85‚Üí`docs/plans/`, renaming it to something meaningful:
    86‚Üí
    87‚Üí```bash
    88‚Üícp ~/.claude/plans/<random-name>.md docs/plans/<issue-id>-<short-name>.md
    89‚Üí```
    90‚Üí
    91‚ÜíExamples:
    92‚Üí- `docs/plans/kapow-a1b-geo-search.md`
    93‚Üí- `docs/plans/auth-refactor.md` (if no issue exists yet)
    94‚Üí
    95‚ÜíUse lowercase kebab-case. Prefer `<issue-id>-<short-name>` when an issue exists.
    96‚Üí
    97‚Üí### Referencing from issues
    98‚Üí
    99‚ÜíAdd a note to the beads issue pointing to the plan:
   100‚Üí
   101‚Üí```bash
   102‚Üíbd update <id> --notes="Plan: docs/plans/<issue-id>-<short-name>.md"
   103‚Üí```
   104‚Üí
   105‚Üí### Resuming work from a plan
   106‚Üí
   107‚ÜíWhen starting a new session, check `docs/plans/` for in-progress plans.
   108‚ÜíIf a beads issue references a plan file, read it before starting work.
   109‚Üí
   110‚Üí### Lifecycle
   111‚Üí
   112‚Üí- Commit and push plan files alongside the code they describe
   113‚Üí- Update plan files as the approach evolves during implementation
   114‚Üí- Delete plan files after the work is fully merged and closed
   115‚Üí
   116‚Üí## Completing an Issue
   117‚Üí
   118‚ÜíAn issue is not complete until:
   119‚Üí1. All code changes pass quality gates (tests, lint, build)
   120‚Üí2. Changes are committed
   121‚Üí3. The issue is closed with `bd close <id>`
   122‚Üí4. Changes are pushed to remote with `bd sync && git push`
   123‚Üí
   124‚Üí## Git Workflow
   125‚Üí
   126‚ÜíWhen work is complete and verified (tests pass, build succeeds, lint is clean),
   127‚Üímerge directly into `main`. Only fall back to a pull request if there is an
   128‚Üíunresolvable problem merging from the command line (e.g. complex conflicts).
   129‚Üí
   130‚Üí### Commit messages and issue references
   131‚Üí
   132‚ÜíWhen a commit includes changes to beads data (`.beads/` files), list all
   133‚Üíaffected issue IDs in the commit message body. This makes it easy to trace
   134‚Üíwhich issues were created, updated, or closed in each push.
   135‚Üí
   136‚ÜíExample:
   137‚Üí```
   138‚ÜíAdd geo search field and update filter UI
   139‚Üí
   140‚ÜíImplements proximity-based search using PostGIS ST_DWithin.
   141‚Üí
   142‚ÜíIssues: kapow-a1b, kapow-c2d, kapow-e3f
   143‚Üí```
   144‚Üí
   145‚Üí### Worktree workflow
   146‚Üí
   147‚ÜíWhen working in a git worktree (e.g. started with `--worktree`), **always merge
   148‚Üíthe branch back into `main` before ending the session.** Do not leave the
   149‚Üíworktree branch unmerged without asking.
   150‚Üí
   151‚Üí**Before merging a worktree branch:**
   152‚Üí- Preview conflicts first: `git merge --no-commit --no-ff <branch>` then `git merge --abort`
   153‚Üí- Rebase long-lived worktrees onto main before merging: `git rebase main` (from the worktree)
   154‚Üí
   155‚Üí**After merging a worktree that touches GraphQL schema files:**
   156‚Üí- Always regenerate: `make api-generate && make web-schema-sync`
   157‚Üí- `.gitattributes` marks generated files with `merge=ours` ‚Äî conflicts auto-resolve to the current branch, but you must regenerate to get the correct content
   158‚Üí
   159‚Üí**Worktree cleanup ‚Äî do NOT do it manually:**
   160‚ÜíClaude Code manages worktree lifecycle automatically when invoked with
   161‚Üí`--worktree`. **Never** run `git worktree remove` or delete the worktree
   162‚Üídirectory yourself ‚Äî the user is prompted to keep or remove it when the session
   163‚Üíends. Manually removing the worktree mid-session can break the working directory
   164‚Üíand cause confusing errors.
   165‚Üí
   166‚Üí## Landing the Plane (Session Completion)
   167‚Üí
   168‚Üí**When ending a work session**, you MUST complete ALL steps below. Work is NOT complete until `git push` succeeds.
   169‚Üí
   170‚Üí**MANDATORY WORKFLOW:**
   171‚Üí
   172‚Üí1. **File issues for remaining work** - Create issues for anything that needs follow-up
   173‚Üí2. **Run quality gates** (if code changed) - Tests, linters, builds
   174‚Üí3. **Update issue status** - Close finished work, update in-progress items
   175‚Üí4. **PUSH TO REMOTE** - This is MANDATORY:
   176‚Üí   ```bash
   177‚Üí   git pull --rebase
   178‚Üí   bd sync
   179‚Üí   git push
   180‚Üí   git status  # MUST show "up to date with origin"
   181‚Üí   ```
   182‚Üí5. **Clean up** - Clear stashes, prune remote branches
   183‚Üí6. **Verify** - All changes committed AND pushed
   184‚Üí7. **Hand off** - Provide context for next session
   185‚Üí
   186‚Üí**CRITICAL RULES:**
   187‚Üí- Work is NOT complete until `git push` succeeds
   188‚Üí- NEVER stop before pushing - that leaves work stranded locally
   189‚Üí- NEVER say "ready to push when you are" - YOU must push
   190‚Üí- If push fails, resolve and retry until it succeeds
   191‚Üí
   192‚Üí
   193‚Üí<!-- BEGIN BEADS INTEGRATION -->
   194‚Üí## Issue Tracking with bd (beads)
   195‚Üí
   196‚Üí**IMPORTANT**: This project uses **bd (beads)** for ALL issue tracking. Do NOT use markdown TODOs, task lists, or other tracking methods.
   197‚Üí
   198‚Üí### Why bd?
   199‚Üí
   200‚Üí- Dependency-aware: Track blockers and relationships between issues
   201‚Üí- Git-friendly: Auto-syncs to JSONL for version control
   202‚Üí- Agent-optimized: JSON output, ready work detection, discovered-from links
   203‚Üí- Prevents duplicate tracking systems and confusion
   204‚Üí
   205‚Üí### Quick Start
   206‚Üí
   207‚Üí**Check for ready work:**
   208‚Üí
   209‚Üí```bash
   210‚Üíbd ready --json
   211‚Üí```
   212‚Üí
   213‚Üí**Create new issues:**
   214‚Üí
   215‚Üí```bash
   216‚Üíbd create "Issue title" --description="Detailed context" -t bug|feature|task -p 0-4 --json
   217‚Üíbd create "Issue title" --description="What this issue is about" -p 1 --deps discovered-from:bd-123 --json
   218‚Üí```
   219‚Üí
   220‚Üí**Claim and update:**
   221‚Üí
   222‚Üí```bash
   223‚Üíbd update bd-42 --status in_progress --json
   224‚Üíbd update bd-42 --priority 1 --json
   225‚Üí```
   226‚Üí
   227‚Üí**Complete work:**
   228‚Üí
   229‚Üí```bash
   230‚Üíbd close bd-42 --reason "Completed" --json
   231‚Üí```
   232‚Üí
   233‚Üí### Issue Types
   234‚Üí
   235‚Üí- `bug` - Something broken
   236‚Üí- `feature` - New functionality
   237‚Üí- `task` - Work item (tests, docs, refactoring)
   238‚Üí- `epic` - Large feature with subtasks
   239‚Üí- `chore` - Maintenance (dependencies, tooling)
   240‚Üí
   241‚Üí### Priorities
   242‚Üí
   243‚Üí- `0` - Critical (security, data loss, broken builds)
   244‚Üí- `1` - High (major features, important bugs)
   245‚Üí- `2` - Medium (default, nice-to-have)
   246‚Üí- `3` - Low (polish, optimization)
   247‚Üí- `4` - Backlog (future ideas)
   248‚Üí
   249‚Üí### Workflow for AI Agents
   250‚Üí
   251‚Üí1. **Check ready work**: `bd ready` shows unblocked issues
   252‚Üí2. **Claim your task**: `bd update <id> --status in_progress`
   253‚Üí3. **Work on it**: Implement, test, document
   254‚Üí4. **Discover new work?** Create linked issue:
   255‚Üí   - `bd create "Found bug" --description="Details about what was found" -p 1 --deps discovered-from:<parent-id>`
   256‚Üí5. **Complete**: `bd close <id> --reason "Done"`
   257‚Üí
   258‚Üí### Auto-Sync
   259‚Üí
   260‚Üíbd automatically syncs with git:
   261‚Üí
   262‚Üí- Exports to `.beads/issues.jsonl` after changes (5s debounce)
   263‚Üí- Imports from JSONL when newer (e.g., after `git pull`)
   264‚Üí- No manual export/import needed!
   265‚Üí
   266‚Üí### Important Rules
   267‚Üí
   268‚Üí- ‚úÖ Use bd for ALL task tracking
   269‚Üí- ‚úÖ Always use `--json` flag for programmatic use
   270‚Üí- ‚úÖ Link discovered work with `discovered-from` dependencies
   271‚Üí- ‚úÖ Check `bd ready` before asking "what should I work on?"
   272‚Üí- ‚ùå Do NOT create markdown TODO lists
   273‚Üí- ‚ùå Do NOT use external issue trackers
   274‚Üí- ‚ùå Do NOT duplicate tracking systems
   275‚Üí
   276‚ÜíFor more details, see README.md and docs/QUICKSTART.md.
   277‚Üí
   278‚Üí<!-- END BEADS INTEGRATION -->
   279‚Üí

1‚Üí## Kapow Monorepo Makefile ‚Äî delegates to component Makefiles
     2‚Üí## Usage: make <target>
     3‚Üí
     4‚Üí# Load .env if present ‚Äî makes vars available to shell commands in recipes
     5‚Üí-include .env
     6‚Üíexport VITE_SUPABASE_URL VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
     7‚Üí
     8‚Üí.PHONY: api-build api-run api-generate api-test api-test-unit \
     9‚Üí        api-lint api-migrate-up api-migrate-down api-clean schema-gen \
    10‚Üí        web-install web-dev web-build web-lint web-clean web-test web-test-coverage web-e2e web-test-e2e web-test-e2e-critical web-screenshots web-storybook \
    11‚Üí        web-schema-sync \
    12‚Üí        lint audit hadolint \
    13‚Üí        smoke-test smoke-test-with-server \
    14‚Üí        api-image-build web-image-build test-image-build test-image-run \
    15‚Üí        tools-build tools-test \
    16‚Üí        dev dev-clean dev-prune dev-db-up dev-db-down \
    17‚Üí        compose-up compose-down compose-logs \
    18‚Üí        test-quick test-standard test-full test-fixture \
    19‚Üí        fixture-sql fixture-json \
    20‚Üí        build-full \
    21‚Üí        spec-gen \
    22‚Üí        demo \
    23‚Üí        help
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üí## api-build: Build the API server binary
    26‚Üíapi-build:
    27‚Üí	$(MAKE) -C api build
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí## build-full: Build single binary with embedded web frontend (API + SPA)
    30‚Üíbuild-full: web-build
    31‚Üí	$(MAKE) -C api build-full
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí## api-run: Run the API server
    34‚Üíapi-run:
    35‚Üí	$(MAKE) -C api run
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí## api-generate: Run GraphQL code generation
    38‚Üíapi-generate:
    39‚Üí	$(MAKE) -C api generate
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí## api-test: Run all API tests
    42‚Üíapi-test:
    43‚Üí	$(MAKE) -C api test
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí## api-test-unit: Run API unit tests
    46‚Üíapi-test-unit:
    47‚Üí	$(MAKE) -C api test-unit
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí## api-lint: Run linter on the API
    50‚Üíapi-lint:
    51‚Üí	$(MAKE) -C api lint
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí## api-migrate-up: Apply database migrations
    54‚Üíapi-migrate-up:
    55‚Üí	$(MAKE) -C api migrate-up
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üí## api-migrate-down: Roll back last database migration
    58‚Üíapi-migrate-down:
    59‚Üí	$(MAKE) -C api migrate-down
    60‚Üí
    61‚Üí## api-clean: Remove API build artifacts
    62‚Üíapi-clean:
    63‚Üí	$(MAKE) -C api clean
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üí## schema-gen: Generate common/schema/schema.sql from migration Up blocks
    66‚Üíschema-gen:
    67‚Üí	bash scripts/gen-schema.sh
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí## web-install: Install web frontend dependencies
    70‚Üíweb-install:
    71‚Üí	$(MAKE) -C web install
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí## web-dev: Start Vite dev server (port 8080, proxies /graphql ‚Üí :8081)
    74‚Üíweb-dev:
    75‚Üí	$(MAKE) -C web dev
    76‚Üí
    77‚Üí## web-build: Type-check and build web frontend for production
    78‚Üíweb-build:
    79‚Üí	$(MAKE) -C web build
    80‚Üí
    81‚Üí## web-lint: Run ESLint on web frontend
    82‚Üíweb-lint:
    83‚Üí	$(MAKE) -C web lint
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üí## web-clean: Remove web build artifacts and node_modules
    86‚Üíweb-clean:
    87‚Üí	$(MAKE) -C web clean
    88‚Üí
    89‚Üí## web-test: Run web unit tests
    90‚Üíweb-test:
    91‚Üí	$(MAKE) -C web test
    92‚Üí
    93‚Üí## web-test-coverage: Run web unit tests with coverage report
    94‚Üíweb-test-coverage:
    95‚Üí	$(MAKE) -C web test-coverage
    96‚Üí
    97‚Üí## web-e2e: Run Playwright E2E tests for web frontend (starts Vite dev server automatically)
    98‚Üíweb-e2e:
    99‚Üí	$(MAKE) -C web e2e-test
   100‚Üí
   101‚Üí## web-test-e2e: Run all Playwright E2E tests (alias with webServer auto-start)
   102‚Üíweb-test-e2e:
   103‚Üí	$(MAKE) -C web e2e-test
   104‚Üí
   105‚Üí## web-test-e2e-critical: Run only @critical tier E2E tests
   106‚Üíweb-test-e2e-critical:
   107‚Üí	$(MAKE) -C web e2e-test-critical
   108‚Üí
   109‚Üí## web-screenshots: Capture visual baseline screenshots across viewports
   110‚Üíweb-screenshots:
   111‚Üí	$(MAKE) -C web screenshots
   112‚Üí
   113‚Üí## web-storybook: Start Storybook dev server for UI components (port 6006)
   114‚Üíweb-storybook:
   115‚Üí	$(MAKE) -C web storybook
   116‚Üí
   117‚Üí## web-schema-sync: Regenerate web/schema.graphql and graphql-env.d.ts from API SDL files
   118‚Üíweb-schema-sync:
   119‚Üí	cat api/graph/schema/schema.graphql \
   120‚Üí	    api/graph/schema/auth.graphql \
   121‚Üí	    api/graph/schema/data_sources.graphql \
   122‚Üí	    api/graph/schema/echo.graphql \
   123‚Üí	    api/graph/schema/field_names_gen.graphql \
   124‚Üí	    api/graph/schema/institution.graphql \
   125‚Üí	    api/graph/schema/preferences.graphql \
   126‚Üí	    api/graph/schema/search.graphql \
   127‚Üí	    api/graph/schema/search_fields.graphql \
   128‚Üí	    api/graph/schema/similarity.graphql \
   129‚Üí	    api/graph/schema/zip.graphql \
   130‚Üí	    > web/schema.graphql
   131‚Üí	cd web && pnpm exec gql-tada generate-output --tsconfig tsconfig.app.json
   132‚Üí
   133‚Üí## lint: Run all linters (Go + web)
   134‚Üílint: api-lint web-lint
   135‚Üí
   136‚Üí## audit: Run security audit on web dependencies (non-zero exit on high+ severity)
   137‚Üíaudit:
   138‚Üí	cd web && pnpm audit --audit-level=high
   139‚Üí
   140‚Üí## hadolint: Lint all Dockerfiles with hadolint (runs via Docker)
   141‚Üíhadolint:
   142‚Üí	@find . -name 'Dockerfile*' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -not -path '*/dist/*' | sort | \
   143‚Üí		while read -r f; do \
   144‚Üí			echo "Linting $$f"; \
   145‚Üí			docker run --rm -i hadolint/hadolint < "$$f" || exit 1; \
   146‚Üí		done
   147‚Üí
   148‚Üí## smoke-test: Run search smoke test against localhost:8081 (requires running API)
   149‚Üísmoke-test:
   150‚Üí	bash scripts/smoke-test.sh
   151‚Üí
   152‚Üí## smoke-test-with-server: Build API, start it, run smoke test, tear down (requires DATABASE_URL)
   153‚Üísmoke-test-with-server:
   154‚Üí	bash scripts/smoke-test-with-server.sh
   155‚Üí
   156‚Üí## api-image-build: Build the standalone API production Docker image
   157‚Üíapi-image-build:
   158‚Üí	docker build -f api/Dockerfile \
   159‚Üí		--build-arg VERSION=$$(git describe --tags --always --dirty 2>/dev/null || echo dev) \
   160‚Üí		--build-arg BUILD_TIME=$$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) \
   161‚Üí		-t kapow-api \
   162‚Üí		api/
   163‚Üí
   164‚Üí## web-image-build: Build the standalone web frontend Docker image (nginx + Vite build)
   165‚Üíweb-image-build:
   166‚Üí	docker build \
   167‚Üí		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_URL=$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL") \
   168‚Üí		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY") \
   169‚Üí		-t kapow-web web/
   170‚Üí
   171‚Üí## test-image-build: Build the all-in-one test Docker image (postgres+API+nginx)
   172‚Üítest-image-build: hadolint
   173‚Üí	docker build \
   174‚Üí		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_URL=$$VITE_SUPABASE_URL") \
   175‚Üí		$$([ -n "$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY" ] && echo "--build-arg VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=$$VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY") \
   176‚Üí		-t kapow-test .
   177‚Üí
   178‚Üí## test-image-run: Run the test image, exposing the web UI on http://localhost:8080
   179‚Üítest-image-run: test-image-build
   180‚Üí	docker run --rm -p 8080:80 kapow-test
   181‚Üí
   182‚Üí## demo: Build test image, run it, and launch the interactive Playwright demo
   183‚Üídemo:
   184‚Üí	bash scripts/demo.sh $(DEMO_ARGS)
   185‚Üí
   186‚Üí## dev: Start full-stack dev environment (PostgreSQL + API + web) with hot reload
   187‚Üídev:
   188‚Üí	bash scripts/dev.sh
   189‚Üí
   190‚Üí## dev-clean: Remove this checkout's dev database volume (forces fresh fixture load on next `make dev`)
   191‚Üídev-clean:
   192‚Üí	@HASH=$$(echo "$$PWD" | md5sum | cut -c1-8); \
   193‚Üí	VOL="kapow-dev-pgdata-$$HASH"; \
   194‚Üí	CONT="kapow-dev-$$HASH"; \
   195‚Üí	docker rm -f "$$CONT" 2>/dev/null || true; \
   196‚Üí	if docker volume inspect "$$VOL" >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
   197‚Üí		docker volume rm "$$VOL"; \
   198‚Üí		echo "Removed volume $$VOL"; \
   199‚Üí	else \
   200‚Üí		echo "Volume $$VOL does not exist"; \
   201‚Üí	fi
   202‚Üí
   203‚Üí## dev-db-up: Start standalone dev database (PostgreSQL+PostGIS on port 5433, full dataset)
   204‚Üídev-db-up:
   205‚Üí	docker compose -f compose/db.yml up -d --wait
   206‚Üí	@echo ""
   207‚Üí	@echo "Dev database starting on port 5433. Use:"
   208‚Üí	@echo "  DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/kapow?sslmode=disable"
   209‚Üí
   210‚Üí## dev-db-down: Stop and remove the standalone dev database container
   211‚Üídev-db-down:
   212‚Üí	docker compose -f compose/db.yml down
   213‚Üí
   214‚Üí## compose-up: Start 3-service production topology (db + api + web) on http://localhost:8080
   215‚Üícompose-up:
   216‚Üí	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml up -d --build --wait
   217‚Üí
   218‚Üí## compose-down: Stop and remove compose services and volumes
   219‚Üícompose-down:
   220‚Üí	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml down -v
   221‚Üí
   222‚Üí## compose-logs: Tail logs from all compose services
   223‚Üícompose-logs:
   224‚Üí	docker compose -f compose/compose.yml logs -f
   225‚Üí
   226‚Üí## dev-prune: Remove kapow-dev volumes older than 30 days
   227‚Üídev-prune:
   228‚Üí	@CUTOFF=$$(date -d '30 days ago' +%s 2>/dev/null || date -v-30d +%s); \
   229‚Üí	for vol in $$(docker volume ls --filter label=kapow-dev=true -q 2>/dev/null); do \
   230‚Üí		CREATED=$$(docker volume inspect "$$vol" --format '{{.CreatedAt}}' 2>/dev/null); \
   231‚Üí		VOL_TS=$$(date -d "$$CREATED" +%s 2>/dev/null || date -j -f "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S" "$$CREATED" +%s 2>/dev/null || echo 0); \
   232‚Üí		if [ "$$VOL_TS" -lt "$$CUTOFF" ]; then \
   233‚Üí			echo "Removing old volume: $$vol (created $$CREATED)"; \
   234‚Üí			docker volume rm "$$vol" 2>/dev/null || true; \
   235‚Üí		fi; \
   236‚Üí	done; \
   237‚Üí	echo "Prune complete."
   238‚Üí
   239‚Üí## tools-build: Build all tools (samplesql)
   240‚Üítools-build:
   241‚Üí	cd tools/samplesql && go build -o ../../bin/samplesql .
   242‚Üí
   243‚Üí## tools-test: Run tests for all tools
   244‚Üítools-test:
   245‚Üí	cd tools/samplesql && go test ./...
   246‚Üí
   247‚Üí## test-quick: Fast checks ‚Äî Go unit tests, TS type-check + build, ESLint (~10-20s, no DB)
   248‚Üítest-quick:
   249‚Üí	MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh api-test-unit web-build web-lint
   250‚Üí
   251‚Üí## test-standard: Full local validation ‚Äî quick + web unit tests + Go lint (~30-60s, no DB)
   252‚Üítest-standard:
   253‚Üí	$(MAKE) test-quick && MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh web-test api-lint
   254‚Üí
   255‚Üí## test-fixture: Validate fixture data loads cleanly against current schema (spins up temp Docker PG)
   256‚Üítest-fixture:
   257‚Üí	bash scripts/test-fixture.sh
   258‚Üí
   259‚Üí## test-full: Complete validation ‚Äî standard + integration, e2e, smoke, fixture (requires DB + running services)
   260‚Üítest-full:
   261‚Üí	$(MAKE) test-standard && MAKE=$(MAKE) bash scripts/parallel-run.sh api-test test-fixture tools-test && $(MAKE) web-test-e2e && $(MAKE) smoke-test
   262‚Üí
   263‚Üí## fixture-sql: Generate complete.sql.xz from JSON fixture data (requires Docker)
   264‚Üífixture-sql:
   265‚Üí	bash scripts/gen-fixture-sql.sh
   266‚Üí
   267‚Üí## fixture-json: Re-extract JSON fixture data from a live database (requires DATABASE_URL)
   268‚Üífixture-json:
   269‚Üí	cd tools/samplesql && go run . --format json --output-dir ../../common/src/main/resources/data/tables
   270‚Üí
   271‚Üí## spec-gen: Generate reference documentation in docs/specs/generated/
   272‚Üíspec-gen:
   273‚Üí	bash scripts/gen-specs.sh
   274‚Üí
   275‚Üí## help: List all available targets
   276‚Üíhelp:
   277‚Üí	@ESSENTIAL="api-generate build-full dev help lint test-image-run test-full test-quick test-standard web-schema-sync"; \
   278‚Üí	echo ""; \
   279‚Üí	echo "Usage: make <target>"; \
   280‚Üí	echo ""; \
   281‚Üí	echo "Essential targets:"; \
   282‚Üí	echo ""; \
   283‚Üí	grep -hE '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
   284‚Üí		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
   285‚Üí			for e in $$ESSENTIAL; do \
   286‚Üí				if [ "$$target" = "$$e" ]; then \
   287‚Üí					printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
   288‚Üí					break; \
   289‚Üí				fi; \
   290‚Üí			done; \
   291‚Üí		done; \
   292‚Üí	echo ""; \
   293‚Üí	echo "All targets:"; \
   294‚Üí	echo ""; \
   295‚Üí	grep -hE '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
   296‚Üí		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
   297‚Üí			SKIP=false; \
   298‚Üí			for e in $$ESSENTIAL; do \
   299‚Üí				if [ "$$target" = "$$e" ]; then SKIP=true; break; fi; \
   300‚Üí			done; \
   301‚Üí			if [ "$$SKIP" = "false" ]; then \
   302‚Üí				printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
   303‚Üí			fi; \
   304‚Üí		done
   305‚Üí

content.text:
Perfect. I've gathered all the requested information. Here's a summary:

## Summary of Beads Configuration and Project Documentation

### 1. `.beads/config.yaml`
The Beads configuration enables:
- **no-db mode**: Uses `.beads/issues.jsonl` as the source of truth instead of a Dolt database
- Default settings are minimal ‚Äî most other fields are commented out
- Key commented-out features include:
  - `sync-branch`: For team projects (not currently set)
  - Multi-repo hydration (experimental)
  - Integration settings: Jira, Linear, GitHub (stored in database, not in file)

### 3. `AGENTS.md` (Beads Integration Section)
Comprehensive guidelines including:
- **Quick commands** (lines 7-12): `bd ready`, `bd show`, `bd update`, `bd close`, `bd sync`

- **Issue title guidelines** (lines 15-29):

  - Under 50 characters total

- **Issue types** (lines 33-37):

  - `feature` ‚Äî new user-visible capability
  - `task` ‚Äî scaffold/chore/infrastructure
  - `bug` ‚Äî broken functionality

- **Creating epics & child issues** (lines 39-63):
  - Epics use `--waits-for-gate all-children`
  - Children use `--parent` and `--waits-for` flags

- **Completing issues** (lines 116-121):
  - Tests pass, lint clean, build succeeds
  - Commit changes
  - Close with `bd close`
  - Push with `bd sync && git push`

- **Landing the plane** (lines 166-191):
